vBRIEFInfo.description: Adopt .deft/core/ as canonical install layout (closes the deft/ vs .deft/core/ contract divergence currently breaking states B/C/D installs from webinstaller/sync skill/oz). Single v0.27 release line: rc1 (validation vehicle) -> GA (after rc1 dogfood is clean). Three coordinated workstreams: (PR1) contract-string flip across templates/agents-entry.md, main.md, all skills, README, UPGRADING, QUICK-START, docs + managed-section marker bump v1 -> v2; (PR2) scripts/relocate.py wipe-and-reinstall relocator + tasks/relocate.yml + webinstaller-hosted upgrade.sh/upgrade.ps1 bootstrap scripts + state A-G synthetic-fixture test matrix; (PR3) cmd_gate state-detection (A/B/C/D) auto-prompt + UPGRADING.md '## From deft/ -> .deft/core/' section + dogfood harness. Drops the ./deft/ override directory from #11 -- single namespace, no override merge semantics in v0.27. Closes #992 on GA; #176, #986, #989, #990 closeable on GA. PowerShell 5.1 non-ASCII rule (#798) governs all file edits touching contract-string docs (em dashes, arrows).

plan.title: feat(installer): adopt .deft/core/ as canonical install layout; ship relocator and contract flip in v0.27 (#992)

narratives.Problem: Directive's documented install layout (deft/) and the actual output of every current installer (.deft/core/) have diverged. Inventory across 38 deftai repos identified four install states: A pure deft/ (working; legacy installs); B pure .deft/core/ (broken; produced by current webinstaller, sync skill, oz -- AGENTS.md says deft/ but framework lives at .deft/core/); C hybrid both dirs (broken; agents follow whichever they read first); D AGENTS.md only (broken partial install). State A is the only working configuration today and B/C/D are accumulating with every new install. The contract is broken in the direction directive should fix, not the direction installers should change. Root-cause search of changelog/git/issue trail surfaced no directive-side declaration of .deft/core/ as canonical -- the only deliberate-design appearance is #11 (npm/pip CLI proposal, 2026-02-04) which spec'd .deft/core/ for read-only packaged assets, .deft/cache/ for gitignored cache, and ./deft/ for project overrides. Installers picked up the .deft/core/ path from #11 but skipped the ./deft/ override directory, producing the half-implemented hybrid consumers see today. #768 later canonized deft/ as the visible framework root via templates/agents-entry.md and AGENTS.md managed-section markers, but installer behavior had already drifted.
narratives.Overview: Three coordinated workstreams shipping as a single v0.27 release line. (1) PR1 contract flip -- string swap deft/run -> .deft/core/run across templates/agents-entry.md, main.md, legacy redirect stubs, every skills/**/*.md, README, UPGRADING, QUICK-START, docs/**; bump managed-section marker v1 -> v2 so cmd_gate fires agents-md=stale automatically on every current install; matching test updates pinned. (2) PR2 wipe-and-reinstall relocator -- new scripts/relocate.py + tasks/relocate.yml + webinstaller-hosted upgrade.sh / upgrade.ps1 bootstrap; bootstrap fetches fresh framework to a temp dir and runs the relocator FROM that copy (never self-destructive); state A-G synthetic-fixture test matrix; pre-flight hard-fail without --force on customized framework OR active vbrief/active/*.vbrief.json with plan.status running. (3) PR3 cmd_gate auto-prompt -- gate detects state A/B/C/D and surfaces 'Run <framework>/run relocate to upgrade. (Y/n)'; auto-prompt ONLY, never auto-wipe (mirrors #884 ghx-install consent gate); UPGRADING.md '## From deft/ -> .deft/core/' section with four-field micro-format header; dogfood validation harness. Sequencing: PR1 lands first (PR2/PR3 reference new strings); PR2 + PR3 parallelize after PR1 merges; tag v0.27.0-rc.1 once all three are merged + state matrix is green; dogfood across 2-3 deftai repos covering states A/B/C with snapshot-rollback verified at least once; tag v0.27.0 GA on clean dogfood. Tier 2-4 (pre-v0.23 reach) is out-of-band via deft-news / release notes / <webinstaller>/upgrade -- bootstrap is the universal entry point regardless of source state; pre-v0.20 schema migration chains through existing scripts/migrate_vbrief.py (#793).
narratives.Constraint: SCOPE FENCE -- explicit out-of-scope per issue body: ./deft/ override directory from #11 (single-namespace contract for v0.27; override merge semantics deferred to a future architectural decision); Taskfile.deft.yml indirection layer for consumer Taskfile (deferred); npm/pip rail packaging itself (#11 proceeds in parallel using .deft/core/); rename of deft -> other namespace (#418/#423 territory). PRE-FLIGHT HARD-FAILS: relocator MUST refuse without --force when (a) framework dir is git-tracked and customized (print preserved-files list), OR (b) any vbrief/active/*.vbrief.json has plan.status == running (active swarm work). NO HARD-FAIL ON STATE A IN v0.27: gate prompts to relocate but legacy deft/ keeps working until a future cycle decides otherwise. PowerShell 5.1 + non-ASCII rule (#798): contract-string docs contain em dashes / arrows / smart quotes -- all file edits MUST go through Python pathlib + UTF-8; Get-Content -Raw / Set-Content / inline -replace forbidden. Marker v1 -> v2 bump intentionally fires agents-md=stale on every current install -- surface in rc1 + GA release notes. Bootstrap is NEVER self-destructive: it fetches fresh framework to a temp dir and runs the relocator from THAT copy, so the in-place framework about to be wiped never executes its own wipe. Auto-prompt NEVER auto-wipe: operator consent required at every gate-prompt invocation. CI / external tooling that hardcodes deft/run is out of framework's control -- relocator does an advisory grep -r 'deft/run' over consumer files outside .deft/core/ and prints findings (no auto-fix).
narratives.Outcome: scripts/relocate.py (new): wipe-and-reinstall implementation idempotent across states A/B/C/D; snapshot-rollback path; pre-flight hard-fails for --force gate (customized framework, active swarm). tasks/relocate.yml (new): wires `task relocate`. webinstaller-hosted upgrade.sh + upgrade.ps1 bootstrap (in webinstaller repo, not this repo): fetches fresh framework to temp + runs relocator from there. templates/agents-entry.md, main.md, all skills/**/*.md, README, UPGRADING, QUICK-START, docs/**: contract strings flipped deft/ -> .deft/core/. Managed-section marker bumped v1 -> v2 (cmd_gate fires agents-md=stale automatically on every current install). cmd_gate state-detection (A/B/C/D) + auto-prompt 'Run <framework>/run relocate to upgrade. (Y/n)'. UPGRADING.md '## From deft/ -> .deft/core/' section with four-field micro-format header. tests/relocate/* covering state A-G synthetic-fixture matrix + pre-flight hard-fail tests + cmd_gate state-detection unit tests + contract-string regression test. CHANGELOG.md [Unreleased] entries across all 3 PRs. v0.27.0-rc.1 tagged once all 3 PRs merged + state matrix green. Dogfood pass: 2-3 deftai repos relocated end-to-end across states A/B/C; snapshot rollback verified at least once. Webinstaller / sync skill / oz / Go-installer (#636) verified conformant against rc1. v0.27.0 GA tagged after rc1 dogfood is clean. Release notes include Tier 2-4 bootstrap one-liner for out-of-band reach. Issues #176, #986, #989, #990 closed on v0.27.0 GA (each with comment referencing closing PR / release).
narratives.Test: tests/relocate/test_state_matrix.py (new): synthetic state A-G fixtures (A pure deft/, B pure .deft/core/, C hybrid both dirs, D AGENTS.md only, E customized framework dir, F missing vbrief/, G active swarm worktree with running plan.status); each fixture runs relocator -> assert end state (.deft/core/ present, deft/ removed unless preserved-files-list, AGENTS.md re-rendered with marker v2, .gitignore updated to include .deft/core/, snapshot-rollback path callable). tests/relocate/test_preflight.py (new): --force required when (a) customized framework + (b) active swarm; both hard-fail without --force; preserved-files list printed. tests/cmd_gate/test_state_detection.py (extended): A/B/C/D unit tests; correct prompt emitted; never auto-wipes. tests/contract/test_no_legacy_deft_run.py (new): regex-grep regression -- no `deft/run` references remain outside legacy redirect stubs after PR1. PowerShell 5.1 mojibake regression: relocator round-trips a fixture file containing em dashes, arrows, smart quotes (#798) -- bytes preserved exactly. task check green across PR1 + PR2 + PR3. Dogfood acceptance: rc1 relocator ran end-to-end on 2-3 deftai repos covering states A, B, C with at least one snapshot-rollback verification.
narratives.DesignChoice: WIPE-NOT-DIFF-MERGE for the relocator: one code path that is idempotent across states A/B/C/D, enforces #11's read-only-packaged-assets contract, has a trivial test surface (state matrix x relocator -> assert end state), catches stale framework versions for free (state B benefits even though no path move is needed), and aligns with npm/pip rail semantics when #11 ships. SINGLE-NAMESPACE not dual-dir-with-overrides: drop ./deft/ overrides from the #11 proposal because override merge semantics are a separate architectural decision; v0.27 ships the simpler single-namespace contract and overrides can be revisited in a future cycle. RC1 -> GA SEQUENCING: v0.27.0-rc.1 is the validation vehicle (full implementation built and tagged); v0.27.0 GA only tagged on clean rc1 dogfood across states A/B/C. NO HARD-FAIL ON STATE A IN v0.27: gate prompts to relocate but legacy deft/ keeps working -- a future cycle can decide whether to deprecate the legacy path. CONTRACT-STRINGS-FIRST PR ORDER: PR1 lands first because PR2 and PR3 both reference the new strings and the marker v2 contract; PR2 (relocator) and PR3 (gate prompt) parallelize after PR1 merges. AUTO-PROMPT NEVER AUTO-WIPE: gate surfaces the prompt but operator must consent (mirrors task setup ghx-install consent gate from #884). BOOTSTRAP NEVER SELF-DESTRUCTIVE: bootstrap fetches fresh framework to a temp dir and runs the relocator from THAT copy, so the in-place framework about to be wiped never executes its own wipe. ADVISORY-NOT-AUTOFIX FOR EXTERNAL HARDCODES: relocator greps for `deft/run` outside .deft/core/ and prints findings; CI / external tooling that hardcodes the legacy path is out of framework's control and is not auto-rewritten. SINGLE-SCOPE-NOT-THREE-SIBLINGS: the 3-PR split is an internal sequencing concern, not a scope boundary -- items are tightly coupled by the marker v1 -> v2 contract and ship as one v0.27.0 release line; tracking as one scope vBRIEF with 3 PR-shaped items[] is the cleaner cohort model.
narratives.Origin: Ingested from https://github.com/deftai/directive/issues/992 via task triage:accept (audit-log decision_id 0c936d15-90d3-423f-8080-5573f4d4be92).

items.title: PR1 -- contract strings flipped (deft/run -> .deft/core/run) across templates/agents-entry.md, main.md, legacy redirect stubs, every skills/**/*.md, README.md, UPGRADING.md, QUICK-START.md, docs/**; managed-section marker bumped v1 -> v2 so cmd_gate fires agents-md=stale on every current install; matching test updates pinned. PR1 lands first because PR2 + PR3 reference the new strings and marker v2 contract.

PR2 -- scripts/relocate.py (new wipe-and-reinstall implementation, idempotent across states A/B/C/D, snapshot-rollback path, pre-flight hard-fails for --force gate); tasks/relocate.yml (new, wires task relocate); webinstaller-hosted upgrade.sh + upgrade.ps1 bootstrap scripts (in webinstaller repo; fetch fresh framework to temp + run relocator from there); state A-G synthetic-fixture test matrix; pre-flight hard-fail tests for customized framework + active swarm.

PR3 -- cmd_gate state-detection (A/B/C/D) + auto-prompt 'Run <framework>/run relocate to upgrade. (Y/n)' (auto-prompt only, never auto-wipe); UPGRADING.md '## From deft/ -> .deft/core/' section with four-field micro-format header; dogfood validation harness; cmd_gate state-detection unit tests.

Tag v0.27.0-rc.1 once PR1 + PR2 + PR3 are merged and state-matrix tests are green. rc1 release notes call out the marker v1 -> v2 drift (every current install fires agents-md=stale on first gate invocation post-v0.27 -- intended).
Dogfood pass against rc1 -- 2-3 deftai repos relocated end-to-end across states A, B, and C; snapshot rollback verified at least once. Dogfood gate is the precondition for v0.27.0 GA.
Webinstaller / sync skill / oz / Go-installer (#636) verified conformant against rc1 -- each producing .deft/core/ layout matching the new contract strings + marker v2. Conformance failure blocks GA.
Tag v0.27.0 GA after rc1 dogfood is clean. Release notes include Tier 2-4 bootstrap one-liner for pre-v0.23 out-of-band reach (deft-news / release notes / <webinstaller>/upgrade).

Close #176 (webinstaller hold lifted), #986 (install-layout contract resolved), #989 (sync skill layout re-scoped to verify-conformance), #990 (auto-repair-on-upgrade subsumed by relocator) on v0.27.0 GA -- each with comment referencing the closing PR / release.

PR4 -- CLI / Taskfile / events.json contract-string flip (post-PR1 follow-up). 9 deft/run references in non-doc surfaces: 7 in run, 1 in tasks/framework.yml comment, 1 in events/registry.json event description. Removes the corresponding exclusions from tests/contract/test_no_legacy_deft_run.py.

x-tracking.branch_strategy: 3-PR cohort -- feat/992a-contract-strings (PR1, lands first), feat/992b-relocator (PR2, parallelizes after PR1), feat/992c-gate-prompt-upgrading (PR3, parallelizes after PR1)

x-tracking.ps51_non_ascii_note: Contract-string docs contain em dashes / arrows / smart quotes -- per AGENTS.md PowerShell section + #798, all file edits MUST go through Python pathlib + UTF-8. Get-Content -Raw / Set-Content / inline -replace forbidden on PS 5.1.
x-tracking.marker_bump_warning: Managed-section marker v1 -> v2 intentionally fires agents-md=stale on every current install on first cmd_gate invocation post-v0.27 -- this is HOW operators get the prompt; surface in rc1 + GA release notes.

references.title: Issue #992: Adopt .deft/core/ as canonical install layout; ship relocator and contract flip in v0.27 (rc1 -> GA)
Issue #11: Publish Deft as an NPM + PIP CLI -- origin of .deft/core/ layout choice (read-only packaged assets); the ./deft/ override directory it spec'd is intentionally dropped from this scope
Issue #176: webinstaller -- currently held; lift hold once contract flip lands (closeable on v0.27.0 GA)
Issue #768: universal upgrade gate + AGENTS.md managed-section contract -- PR1 bumps the marker from v1 to v2
Issue #793: task migrate:preflight / task migrate:vbrief -- chained by relocator for pre-v0.20 schema migration tier
Issue #794: pre-#768 AGENTS.md legacy migration -- _wrap_legacy_in_markers reused by relocator's AGENTS.md re-render path
Issue #845: triage v1 -- .deft-cache/ precedent for hidden-namespace gitignore-default contract; relocator Phase 7 mirrors that gitignore-default convention
Issue #986: install-layout contract -- this issue's parent decision; closeable on v0.27.0 GA
Issue #989: sync skill layout -- re-scoped to 'verify conformance against rc1'; closeable on v0.27.0 GA
Issue #990: auto-repair-on-upgrade -- subsumed by relocator; closeable on v0.27.0 GA
Issue #418: rename discussion -- separate; deferred (#418/#423 territory, not v0.27 scope)
Issue #423: rename discussion -- separate; deferred (#418/#423 territory, not v0.27 scope)
Issue #636: Go-installer -- verified conformant against rc1 as part of acceptance criterion 992-ac-6
Issue #798: PS 5.1 non-ASCII deterministic gate -- governs all contract-string file edits in PR1 (em dashes, arrows, smart quotes)
Issue #884: ghx adoption + task setup consent gate -- referenced by the auto-prompt-never-auto-wipe DesignChoice (operator-consent gate convention)
